questions.q: What is the central theme of Deep Water by Henry Cecil?
questions.explanation: The play revolves around the theme of power and corruption, as the characters struggle for control and manipulation.

questions.q: Who is the primary antagonist in the play Deep Water?
questions.explanation: George Summers is the main antagonist who uses his power and influence to manipulate others and achieve his goals.

questions.q: What is the role of Sir Arthur Groome in the play Deep Water?
questions.explanation: Sir Arthur Groome is a corrupt politician who uses his position to further his own interests and advance his career.

questions.q: What is the significance of the title 'Deep Water' in the play?
opts: (A) It represents the depth of human emotions
(B) It symbolizes the danger of getting involved in politics
(C) It represents the difficulty of relationships
(D) It symbolizes the depth of human corruption
questions.explanation: The title 'Deep Water' represents the depth of human corruption and the danger of getting involved in the corrupt world of politics.

questions.q: What is the relationship between George Summers and Sir Arthur Groome?
questions.explanation: George Summers and Sir Arthur Groome are business partners who work together to further their own interests.

questions.q: What is the role of John Meredith in the play Deep Water?
questions.explanation: John Meredith is a whistleblower who exposes the corruption of George Summers and Sir Arthur Groome.

questions.q: What is the setting of the play Deep Water?
questions.explanation: The play is set in a business office where the characters discuss and manipulate business deals.

questions.q: Who is the protagonist of the play Deep Water?
questions.explanation: John Meredith is the protagonist who tries to expose the corruption of George Summers and Sir Arthur Groome.

questions.q: What is the tone of the play Deep Water?
questions.explanation: The tone of the play is satirical, as it critiques the corrupt world of politics and business.

questions.q: What is the conflict in the play Deep Water?
opts: (B) External conflict between characters and society
(D) A combination of all the above
questions.explanation: The conflict in the play is a combination of personal, external, and internal conflicts between characters and society.

questions.q: Who is the character who is most responsible for the downfall of George Summers?
questions.explanation: John Meredith is the character who exposes the corruption of George Summers, leading to his downfall.

questions.q: What is the role of Lady Summers in the play Deep Water?
questions.explanation: Lady Summers is a loyal friend who tries to protect her husband from the consequences of his actions.

questions.q: What is the significance of the character of Sir John Patterne?
questions.explanation: Sir John Patterne represents the wise judge who tries to bring justice to the corrupt world of politics and business.

questions.q: What is the message of the play Deep Water?
opts: (B) Power and corruption go hand in hand
(C) Loyalty and friendship are more important than wealth and power
questions.explanation: The message of the play is that power and corruption go hand in hand, and that the corrupt world of politics and business is a corrupting influence on individuals.

questions.q: What is the relationship between the play Deep Water and the real world?
opts: (A) It is a realistic portrayal of the real world
(B) It is a satirical commentary on the real world
(C) It is a utopian vision of the ideal world
(D) It is a dystopian portrayal of the worst-case scenario
questions.explanation: The play Deep Water is a satirical commentary on the real world, critiquing the corrupt world of politics and business.

questions.q: What is the character trait of George Summers?
questions.explanation: George Summers is driven by greed and a desire for wealth and power.

questions.q: What is the impact of the play Deep Water on the audience?
opts: (A) It makes the audience feel good
(B) It makes the audience feel bad
(C) It makes the audience question the morality of the characters
questions.explanation: The play Deep Water makes the audience question the morality of the characters and the corrupt world of politics and business.

questions.q: What is the style of the play Deep Water?
questions.explanation: The play Deep Water is written in a satirical style, critiquing the corrupt world of politics and business.

questions.q: Who is the character who is most responsible for the downfall of Sir Arthur Groome?
questions.explanation: John Meredith is the character who exposes the corruption of Sir Arthur Groome, leading to his downfall.

questions.q: What is the significance of the character of Lady Summers?
questions.explanation: Lady Summers represents the loyal friend who tries to protect her husband from the consequences of his actions.
